top_categories.workout_context: When someone attacks on mixed terrain, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Vietnam Cycling Challenge's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before November.
62 miles on mixed terrain burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
79ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
Base fitness for Vietnam Cycling Challenge. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
Vietnam Cycling Challenge's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 62 miles.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Vietnam Cycling Challenge. Train it until it feels boring.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 4,921ft of climbing on mixed terrain does to your power file on race day.
At Vietnam Cycling Challenge, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Double-threshold training builds sustained power with less fatigue. More FTP per hour of training.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on mixed terrain. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Below LT1, you burn fat. Above it, you burn glycogen. For 62 miles, a higher LT1 means slower bonk.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for Vietnam Cycling Challenge’s 62 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.
race_overlay.altitude: Vietnam Cycling Challenge with 4,921ft of climbing, much of it above 8,000ft demands altitude preparation. Arrive 5–7 days early for acclimatization. Expect 5–15% power reduction at altitude. Increase iron intake 4 weeks out. Hydrate aggressively — altitude increases fluid loss by 20–40%.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on VO2max, Race Simulation, and Durability to prepare you for 62 miles of mixed terrain in Da Lat, Lam Dong, Vietnam.
